reviews.author: Josh Dobeck
reviews.text: The fishing village is cool, It's a functioning live fishing village. There's a lot going on in the village itself. It's not big but it's a makeshift village restaurant fish processing on the beach. So all these boats that go out in the morning come back and they break down the fish and they package it to be sold locally or being exported to wherever. There are a variety of little stalls that will prepare fish for you but it is dirty, there is garbage everywhere. There are shells everywhere. It's the way they do their thing but it's just heartbreakingly dirty. It's cool to see though. I would recommend driving past it, take a picture or two but you go down into there. It's funky

reviews.author: Liza Martins (Lizaaroundtheworld)
reviews.text: It’s a photogenic Village, with a lot of rounded boats on the Bay, a nice and alive local market. But it’s too dirty. The sand it’s dirty, the water is dirty. Garbage everywhere. I never saw another beach so dirty! Looks like for centuries they don’t clean the beach and all the fishing process it’s done over this sand. I don’t know. The views are nice in the evenings with all the lights on the boats. But during the day it’s disgusting. Doesn’t call me to go downstairs close to the water at all

reviews.author: Akang
reviews.text: A must stop, best at dawn, a remarkable fishing village, with fantastic view of fishing boats at sea, scenes of fishmen selling their daily fresh catches at the beach is captivating! Not to mention the sun rise and moon from the beach.

reviews.author: Анастасия Дар (Pacificaana)
reviews.text: Go there by taxi, not with excursion The place is authentic, has it's own vibe. You'll find good prices for fresh seafood and try it there. You can easily order fresh shrimps and coffee for less than 300k vnd. Go at 7 am, not that hot and not crowded Tho it's super dirty, rubbish is everywhere.

reviews.author: Athanasius Ooi Xiehui
reviews.text: Walked over to the fishing village after spotting it with my drone. I went in the evening so might be a different experience if you go in the morning. But in the evening there is a covered section that sells live seafood which was quite interesting. Managed to try a fish, 4 lobsters and some snails. It was a good experience but for some it can be quite dirty as they throw the shells of the seafood on the beach.

reviews.author: Catherine
reviews.text: I have been wanting to come here so much and I was so disappointed. The social media doesn’t show the reality of this place and it’s disgusting. The place is an actual dumpster, so much trash and dirt. How do people work or live here, I have no idea but I’m glad I don’t. Not sure I would even want to buy any fish here. Very saddening place. If you don’t look down and have “this is how it is, whatever” attitude, it does look cool. Enjoy your trash 🙂

reviews.author: Anna
reviews.text: The fishing village is quite cute, still has a lot of charm despite being a tourist attraction. It is a nice stop if you’re in Mui Ne, to take pictures and see the sunset for example. Worth the time for a day tour!

reviews.author: Erik Borkner
reviews.text: Great little morning trip with a duration for about 90 min from Panadus resort. First fish market on the beach, then a walk to the fish market in town and then return. Good guide that gave us a good insight in this little town.

reviews.author: lliilllil
reviews.text: Smart engineers of Vietnam! 🙏Please make a pulley system for trash from the beach!! Fun and cute design!!! So much trash down there because no one wants to bring them up to the streets where there are loads if trash can!

reviews.author: Dawid Wiewiora
reviews.text: everything looks beautiful until you go down to the beach, how can they live like that?!

reviews.author: Bogdan Stepien
reviews.text: One star for trash everywhere. Fishing village people, seriously? Do you really have to dump everything including plastics and organic waste right onto this beautiful beach?

reviews.author: Jensen Chua Photography
reviews.text: A signature "attraction" of Mui Ne. This venue is not set up for tourists but a real local fish market. The area has plenty of trash strewn everywhere, but don't let it distract from the evolving authentic slice of daily. Ideally, go at sunrise time (533am) and feel the place comes alive. A photographer's heaven indeed. By 645am, the activities would have thin out by then. With waking up early for this gem. No entrance free needed.

reviews.author: Ashley T (Shark and Eagle)
reviews.text: It is what it is, it’s not the best for its view but it’s authentic Vietnamese village people’s life. I wish I could speak Vietnamese to have a chat with people live here.

reviews.author: Andy Shin
reviews.text: We visited here after our morning sand dune tour and loved seeing the local life. It was great to see people selling fresh fish, crabs, and more. It’s a beautiful area; my son had a wonderful time catching and touching sea snails, crabs, and shellfish. ​In hindsight, I wish we had visited on our own rather than with a tour so we could have spent more time exploring the beach. The only downside is that there is quite a bit of washed-up debris, which isn't great, but generally speaking, it's still a very nice spot for photos.

reviews.author: Remko
reviews.text: Beautiful place to see the boats during the day.

reviews.author: kelly fan
reviews.text: The best spot to get a view of the fish village is right in front of a seafood restaurant. Unfortunately, the sightseeing balcony is currently under construction. But don't worry, many tourists are still hopping on and off their Jeeps just to snap a quick picture. Trust me though, it's totally worth stopping by for a bit. While you're here, why not grab a cup of their famous hot coffee( Vietnamese dripping coffee)？ Just sit back, relax, and enjoy the stunning views of the noon and sunset. It's absolutely beautiful!

reviews.author: Lionel Ling
reviews.text: It used to be when you climbed down the stairs you'll get cheap and good seafood. The stall owners who run it now seems to have swapped a lot of fresh seafood with long dead or clearly spoilt seafood, and they did this 2 days in a row when we patronised them. We thought the first day was a misunderstanding, but after a whole container of spoilt mantis shrimps, a whole plate of hollowed out crabs and a 2nd day of the same, it is clear these people are unrepentant cheaters. Take your chances elsewhere!

reviews.author: Vojtěch Ott
reviews.text: Well, ugly beach with garbage amd dogs. Thr boats looks ok from the balcony.

reviews.author: Ryan Viet
reviews.text: Chill place to see sunset and enjoy the view of fishing boats

reviews.author: suraj singh
reviews.text: Mùi Nè Fisherman Village in Phan Thiết offers a unique and authentic coastal experience, perfect for seafood lovers and those interested in experiencing traditional fishing village life. The village is known for its incredibly fresh seafood, with dishes made from fish, shrimp, crabs, squid, and shellfish, all prepared in a traditional style that highlights the natural flavors of the sea. The setting is peaceful and scenic, with a rustic, natural atmosphere where guests can enjoy their meals while taking in beautiful views of the coastline. The service is warm and friendly, often provided by locals, which adds to the genuine, welcoming experience. While the seafood is high quality, it tends to be more expensive than in city restaurants, though most visitors find the prices reasonable given the freshness and taste. Overall, Mùi Nè Fisherman Village is a great destination for those looking to enjoy fresh seafood in an authentic, peaceful setting, offering a more local and less touristy experience.

reviews.author: Egyptian Anubis
reviews.text: Normal place just you will get pic from top

reviews.author: Elisha Wang
reviews.text: Mũi Né Fishing Village at sunrise was an incredible experience. We arrived at 5:30 a.m., which is the best time to see the market in full swing. Fishermen were still bringing in their catch, while women sorted, cleaned, and packed the seafood onto motorbikes for distribution. It felt raw and real—a glimpse into daily life here. As the morning went on, the market quieted, and locals arrived to buy fresh seafood from stalls set up right on the sand. A must-visit, especially for photographers! Easily my favorite experience in Mũi Né.

reviews.author: Jordy Meow (Offbeat Japan)
reviews.text: Garbage everywhere, dogs peeing on my elegant bag, violent sun hitting my poor cute head… at first, the market is not welcoming! But it’s not as touristy as I thought it would be! A real (and somehow frightening) glance into the local life.

reviews.author: tarah shaw
reviews.text: Had a 15min stop here in our tour Incredible Lovely views of the busy water ways with hundreds of boats Great photo opportunity Very nice

reviews.author: Tony Haton
reviews.text: Just the magical place. Smell the salt air, mingle with friendly locals and watch the fishing boats by night. Fanbloodytactic!

reviews.author: Cooper Ng
reviews.text: Very beautiful when you saw many ship boat on the sea, it was amazing. But on the other side, when you near the beach there are many dead fishes and prawns which is very dirty. Their also sell variety seafood at there, I think if you brought they will help you to cook too.

reviews.author: Misko Kordic
reviews.text: a beautiful and unique place to visit regardless of the huge amount of trash on the beach and in the sea. it's unfortunate that no one takes care and cleans up the trash. restaurants are too expensive with prices for tourists they buy fish at very low prices from fishermen. definitely worth a stop in passing.

reviews.author: Joe Davey
reviews.text: An interesting little place, definitely worth a visit if you're nearby. Try ands get there for round four so that you can see it in action. You'll see the fishermen delivering their fresh catch to shore and putting it on show for sale right there! Stick around for an amazing sunset too (pictures do no justice at all!). Not a full 5 stars because they don't look after the beach very well... It's quite a smelly mess.

reviews.author: Steffen Sonnenschein
reviews.text: The Mui Ne Fishing Village in Vietnam is a wonderful place to visit.The local market provides an opportunity to explore the culture with delicious seafood, spices, and souvenirs available for purchase. Watching fishermen trade their freshly caught fish is an interesting experience that can be seen from nearby restaurants or from far away picturesque locations. For those looking for picture perfect moments there are many colorful boats, palm trees, and sand dunes to capture in photographs. All together this quaint fishing village makes a great destination spot for anyone traveling through Vietnam!

reviews.author: Alice
reviews.text: The most interesting thing here is taking the traditional bowl boat here! There are so many bowl boat in this fishing village. They take the boat to float on the sea to go fishing. We pay 150,000VND /a boat to the fisher man for 20mins to have a experience.(we know it’s really expensive so guys if you want to take it remember to bargain!) After that we buy some seafood and have the kitchen nearby to cook it for us. Not bad Not expensive. Same thing, remember to bargain and buy the seafood which are alive.
